scenario: burnout from chronic overwork with no boundaries

turns.user_message: Hey. So I've been struggling with work-life balance. Actually, there is no balance. It's all work and I'm falling apart.
I know, right? It sounds simple when I say it out loud but it doesn't feel simple.
It's been going on for a while, honestly. I just kept pushing it aside.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with no boundaries and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
I don't know. Some days are better than others. Today is a harder day.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about no boundaries and I should just deal with it.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]

I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you actually listened without trying to fix it immediately.

[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life no boundaries has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.

I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
